mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, which directly increases S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a t-stat greater than 1.5, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5d returns during post-shock recovery periods, with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 265.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is detrended.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate falls indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict 5d returns with a t-stat greater than 1.5, or the signal reverses direction during periods of high market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines for two consecutive months, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output that reduces near-term earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during late-cycle expansions.
